query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**Most recent data (July 8–9, 2026): Foreigners REVERSED to net BUYING**
- July 8: +343.6 billion won
- July 9: +134.8 billion won
- This snapped a **13-session selling streak** (June 19–July 7), coinciding with the KOSPI finding a floor near 7,200 (closed 7,291.91 on July 9).

**The dominant 2026 trend — record "Sell Korea" wave:**
- **H1 2026 (Jan–Jun 30): –148.32 trillion won ($95B)** net selling — a record
- **YTD through July 3: –157.3 trillion won ($102.5B)**
- ~90% concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK hynix

**The paradox:** All this selling occurred while the KOSPI rallied ~92% YTD and hit an all-time high of ~9,114 on June 22. Foreign ownership share actually *rose* to 40.47% (from 36.65% in January) because Samsung/SK hynix price gains outweighed the value sold.

**Drivers:** Profit-taking/rebalancing after the ~101% surge, a weakening won, and dollar strength. KRX's CEO framed it as rebalancing, not lost confidence. KB Securities flagged up to ~260 trillion won more potential selling if ownership normalizes toward 35%.

**Important caveat:** No single consolidated "weekly" net figure was published by KRX — the data available are granular daily prints plus cumulative H1/YTD totals. The most notable recent development is the **two-day reversal from record selling to net buying** in early July, though brokerages remain split on whether H2 selling resumes.

Full structured output with all data points and sources has been recorded.
